title: Personal activity log
excerpt: My request is that a personal activity log (specific to a single user) is created. The user would be able to view a history of changes or actions that they have made over several boards or workspaces. It could be a way t…

posts.author: sgalley (Steven Galley)
posts.cookedHtml: My request is that a personal activity log (specific to a single user) is created. The user would be able to view a history of changes or actions that they have made over several boards or workspaces. It could be a way to see every single action performed, be it updates, status changes, item creation etc. This idea came about when I was trying to remember a certain project I was working on and wanted to see what I had done but I couldn’t find what specific action I that I had taken.

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Steven! We’ve developed Master Activity Logs, which acts as a ‘Master Activity Log’ for user activities in multiple boards. It offers advanced reporting based on that data as well. Hopefully that helps!

posts.author: Susana (Susana)
posts.cookedHtml: This will be awesome! I do not know why nobody have mentioned this before, I find it really basic. Hope monday implements it soon.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @sgalley ,
I want to recommend the Board Email Reports app for your case.
It aggregates the changes in the activity log by the column you choose and over the period you choose in the automation rule, and sends the xls-report to any people you mention in the rule (no guest access needed!).
Report types
On a board/project level:

  1. New and updated tasks
  2. Status updates (here you set up the rule that will show tasks transitioned to done)
  3. Budget, Costs, Revenue changes
  4. Timeline and deadline overdue
  5. Time Tracking changes

On an item/task level:

  • Updates by a specific employee
  • Updates mentioning a specific employee
  • Updates containing attachments
  • Status updates
  • Last updates

In you specific case, you need to set up automations on the boards you want to track your actions. I’ve highlighted them in bold.
An example for your case:

image1386×555 53 KB

The email will contain the roundup of item updates made by you and a detailed xls-report where you can check updates:
image395×499 21 KB

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be really nice if it retained to function to undo activities. So it would basically only be a certain filter or view because activities are already tracked anyways.
